scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Cafe kiosks, takeaway pickup, and drink customizations are common; short phrases for less ice, no syrup, or takeaway matter most.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Pointing helps, but simple Korean is useful for portion count, not spicy, water, takeaway, and quick menu confirmation.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Showing the destination on your phone is standard; short phrases for take me here, stop here, wait a minute, and payment method matter most.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Use this more for price confirmation and item choice than bargaining; card versus cash follow-through matters more in South Korea.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: Short respectful phrases do a lot of work in Korea; excuse me, thank you, sorry, and polite acknowledgment matter more than casual friendliness.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Convenience stores solve many traveler problems fast: water, tissues, chargers, receipt, bag, and card-payment confirmation.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Reservation lookup, passport handling, check-in, checkout time, luggage storage, and room-problem reporting are the highest-value front-desk moments.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Station names, subway exits, line numbers, and whether something is near here matter more than generic city-center wording.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: Show your screen early; repair phrases for slower speech, repetition, lost items, and calling for help matter more than long explanations.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Keep this light and optional; ordering, directions, payment, and repair phrases matter more than forced conversation in the first pack.

notes: Shared traveler baseline for Korean, reprioritized around polite utility, Hangul show-screen readability, and non-Latin search caution. Fill translation, pronunciation, and audio posture before runtime wiring.
